title: daemon/store.rs: create_space bumps state seq on duplicate
description:
daemon/src/store.rs create_space(): calls bump_state_seq("Space") unconditionally after INSERT OR IGNORE INTO spaces. When the space already exists (INSERT is a no-op), the state sequence is still incremented, causing all connected clients to invalidate their caches and re-fetch for a write that changed nothing.

Root cause: INSERT OR IGNORE silently succeeds without inserting; the code does not check rows_affected() before bumping.

Fix: check query.rows_affected() == 1 before calling bump_state_seq, or switch to INSERT and handle the unique-constraint error explicitly to distinguish create vs. already-exists.
